entries.description: The draw shoulder elevates towards the ear instead of staying low and engaged, preventing proper back tension and creating instability. (Estimated ranges)
root_causes: Trying to muscle the draw with arm
observable_signs: Draw shoulder height significantly increases from setup to anchor
Draw shoulder appears higher than bow shoulder at full draw
entries.score_impact: Leads to inconsistent anchor, poor power transfer, and vertical string blur, causing inconsistent arrow placement.
entries.embed_text: draw anchor draw_shoulder spine draw_shoulder_height_diff 20-50mm draw shoulder shrug lack back tension muscle draw shoulder down back relax neck inconsistent anchor poor power transfer vertical string blur inconsistent arrow placement

entries.description: The draw elbow drops below the line of the arrow, hindering proper back tension and creating an unstable anchor. (Estimated ranges)
root_causes: Relying on arm muscles instead of back muscles
Trying to 'pull' rather than 'push and pull'
observable_signs: Draw elbow significantly lower than the arrow line
coaching_cues: Point your elbow at the wall behind you
entries.score_impact: Prevents consistent back tension, leading to poor release, inconsistent anchor, and erratic arrow grouping.
entries.embed_text: draw anchor aim draw_elbow draw_shoulder draw_elbow_angle 70-90 degrees low draw elbow chicken wing arm muscles poor shoulder rotation elbow up back high elbow poor release inconsistent anchor erratic arrow grouping

entries.description: The bow arm elbow is locked straight or hyperextended, increasing the risk of string slap and creating unnecessary tension. (Estimated ranges)
root_causes: Lack of awareness of elbow position
Trying to reach for the target
observable_signs: Bow arm elbow angle close to 180 degrees or slightly more
coaching_cues: Slight bend in the bow arm
entries.score_impact: Can cause painful string slap, inconsistent bow torque, and lead to horizontal grouping issues.
entries.embed_text: setup draw anchor aim bow_elbow bow_arm_extension_angle 178-185 degrees hyperextended bow arm over-straightening soft elbow slight bend string slap inconsistent bow torque horizontal grouping issues

entries.description: Failure to engage the scapular muscles during the draw and anchor, leading to reliance on arm strength and an unstable hold. (Estimated ranges)
root_causes: Focusing too much on the arm pull
observable_signs: Draw shoulder remains high or forward during the draw
Lack of subtle movement in the shoulder blades during draw
entries.score_impact: Results in a shaky hold, inconsistent anchor, poor release, and wide arrow groups.
entries.embed_text: draw anchor aim draw_shoulder spine draw_shoulder_rise 15-40mm insufficient back tension weak back muscles squeeze shoulder blades pull with back shaky hold inconsistent anchor poor release wide arrow groups

entries.description: The string hand wrist is bent or hooked, leading to inconsistent string contact, anchor point variation, and poor release.
root_causes: Lack of awareness of wrist position
observable_signs: Draw wrist angle is significantly flexed or extended
Draw hand not aligned with the forearm
entries.score_impact: Causes inconsistent string pressure, varying anchor points, and erratic arrow flight, affecting both horizontal and vertical grouping.
entries.embed_text: draw anchor aim draw_wrist draw_hand bent draw wrist gripping string too tightly flat wrist straight wrist inconsistent string pressure varying anchor points erratic arrow flight

entries.description: The head is tilted laterally, making it difficult to achieve a consistent anchor point and sight picture. (Estimated ranges)
root_causes: Trying to bring the head to the string
observable_signs: Head angle deviates significantly from vertical (e.g., > 5 degrees laterally)
coaching_cues: Bring the string to your face, not face to string
entries.score_impact: Leads to inconsistent anchor depth and height, causing vertical grouping errors and difficulty aiming.
entries.embed_text: setup draw anchor aim head spine head_tilt 5-20 degrees head tilt bring head to string head up straight inconsistent anchor depth height vertical grouping errors

entries.description: The archer's torso leans excessively forward or backward, compromising balance, stability, and consistent body alignment. (Estimated ranges)
root_causes: Trying to 'push' the bow or 'pull' the string with the body
observable_signs: Spine angle deviates significantly from vertical (e.g., > 10 degrees forward or backward)
entries.score_impact: Reduces stability, makes consistent anchor difficult, and can lead to inconsistent shot execution and wide grouping.
entries.embed_text: setup draw anchor aim spine hip knee spine_forward_lean 10-30 degrees spine forward backward lean compensating draw weight stand tall vertical posture reduces stability inconsistent anchor wide grouping

entries.description: The string hand consistently returns to the same physical spot on the face/jaw, which is crucial for repeatable arrow trajectory. (Estimated ranges)
root_causes: Lack of focus during the shot sequence
observable_signs: High standard deviation of draw hand position relative to the head across multiple shots
coaching_cues: Feel the same spot every time
entries.score_impact: Directly impacts vertical and horizontal arrow grouping; inconsistency here leads to wide shot dispersion.
entries.embed_text: anchor draw_hand head anchor_point_consistency 10-30mm inconsistent anchor point lack focus rushing shot feel same spot solid anchor vertical horizontal arrow grouping wide shot dispersion

entries.description: Establishing the correct bow arm position, pointing towards the target, before initiating the draw helps ensure a smooth and efficient draw cycle.
observable_signs: Bow arm not pointing towards the target line during setup
Bow shoulder rotated incorrectly during setup
coaching_cues: Point your bow arm at the target
entries.score_impact: Contributes to a less efficient draw, potentially leading to compensation during the draw phase and affecting overall consistency.
entries.embed_text: setup bow_shoulder bow_elbow bow_wrist bow arm pre-draw alignment point bow arm at target align before draw set frame less efficient draw compensation overall consistency

entries.description: Actively depressing and retracting the draw shoulder engages the back muscles, facilitating a strong and stable draw and anchor. (Estimated ranges)
observable_signs: Draw shoulder height remains low and stable relative to bow shoulder
entries.score_impact: Essential for consistent back tension, stable anchor, and clean release, directly impacting accuracy and grouping.
entries.embed_text: draw anchor draw_shoulder spine draw_shoulder_height_diff -10-10mm draw shoulder engagement shoulder down back engage lats create space neck consistent back tension stable anchor clean release accuracy grouping
